Centerpoint Energy ( (CNP) ) has provided an announcement.

CenterPoint Energy has released audited consolidated financial statements for its indirect subsidiary Southern Indiana Gas and Electric Company for the years ended December 31, 2025 and 2024, along with supplementary financial and operational data.

The company emphasized that these materials are being furnished rather than filed under U.S. securities law, meaning they are not subject to Section 18 liability under the Exchange Act and will not be automatically incorporated into any CenterPoint registration statements unless explicitly designated, which limits their direct legal and regulatory impact for investors.

CEI South functions as an integrated utility with regulated transmission, distribution, and related infrastructure, subject to accounting and oversight standards typical for U.S. energy utilities.

data.title: Starz Entertainment Earnings Call Highlights Streaming Momentum
data.description:
Starz Entertainment Corp ((STRZ)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Starz Entertainment Corp struck an upbeat tone on its latest earnings call, emphasizing record streaming growth, a clean beat on profit targets, and a clear path to positive free cash flow in 2026. Management balanced this optimism with candid acknowledgment of linear TV declines, leverage constraints, and reduced subscriber disclosure, but insisted the business is now on a steadier, more predictable footing.

Record Streaming Gains Anchor Growth Story

Starz said its OTT platform reached an all‑time high of 12.7 million subscribers, up 7.6% versus last year. The company added 370,000 domestic OTT subs in the fourth quarter and lifted total U.S. subscribers by 170,000 to 17.6 million, reinforcing streaming as the core driver of future revenue and scale.

OIBDA Beat Underscores Operational Discipline

The company delivered adjusted OIBDA of $204 million for 2025, topping its $200 million outlook by roughly 2%. Management framed the beat as evidence that tighter cost control, more efficient content spending, and improved monetization are now flowing into earnings, even as the business continues to invest in new shows and markets.

Quarterly Momentum in Profit and Revenue

Fourth‑quarter adjusted OIBDA jumped to $56 million, more than doubling sequentially on the back of lower programming amortization, lower ad and marketing spend, and higher revenue. Quarterly revenue reached $323 million, up 60 basis points from the prior period, signaling improving unit economics even amid a promotional quarter.

Path to Cash Flow and Deleveraging in 2026

Management laid out a detailed road map for 2026, targeting unlevered free cash flow of $80 million to $120 million and positive equity free cash flow for the year. They expect to exit 2026 with leverage of about 2.7x, better than prior guidance and down from 2.9x at year‑end 2025, moving toward a 2.5x long‑term goal.

Building Owned Content for Margin Upside

Starz highlighted progress in shifting toward more owned and co‑owned series to improve future margins and royalty economics. It completed production on its first wholly owned series, Fightland, co‑commissioned with Sky, greenlit Power Origins with an 18‑episode order, and launched the Outlander prequel Blood of My Blood.

Distribution and Bundling Expand Reach

The company restructured its Canadian business, pivoting it into a licensing revenue model to simplify economics and reduce risk. Across markets, management is leaning into bundling and expanded distribution partnerships, arguing that bundles widen the addressable market, add net new subscribers, lift revenue, and improve churn.

Hit Franchises Boost Viewing and Sign‑Ups

Programming performance was another bright spot, with content driving engagement and sub growth. The Spartacus revival drew critical acclaim, while Power Book IV: Force Season 3 delivered a 57% in‑season viewership increase, underscoring how established franchises continue to anchor the service and attract new customers.

Linear Weakness and Promotions Pressure Revenue Mix

Despite streaming gains, linear subscribers continued to decline, weighing on the legacy side of the business. Management also flagged that heavy holiday promotions and more multi‑month low‑churn plans hurt near‑term revenue composition, trading immediate dollars for longer‑term subscriber value.

Investor Unease Over Reduced Subscriber Disclosure

Starting with the March 2026 quarter, Starz will no longer provide subscriber counts, removing a key operating metric many investors track closely. While management argues the business is better assessed on cash flow and profitability, the move could raise questions about transparency and complicate valuation work.

Leverage and Liquidity Remain in Focus

The balance sheet was a central topic, with net debt at $589 million and gross debt at $625 million against just $36 million of cash at year‑end. Starz still has an undrawn $150 million revolver, but management acknowledged leverage as a key constraint and reiterated their commitment to driving it down toward 2.5x over time.

Smoothing Out Volatile Cash and Content Cycles

Executives revisited the company’s history of choppy cash flows and earnings patterns tied to legacy studio‑style content timing. They said that as Starz moves further from that model, 2026 should bring more predictable cash and P&L cadence, though some near‑term synchronization between spend and revenue recognition is still required.

Franchise Dependence and Measured Margin Ambitions

Starz is targeting roughly 20% margins by 2028, but expects only modest improvement in 2026, reflecting continued investment. Management acknowledged that near‑term growth and engagement are heavily concentrated in a few key franchises such as Power and Outlander, which introduces performance risk if any of these brands stumble.

Forward Guidance Signals Gradual but Durable Upside

Looking ahead, the company framed 2026 as an inflection year built on OTT revenue growth and at least $200 million of adjusted OIBDA, with low single‑digit growth from the 2025 base. With cash content spending kept under roughly $650 million and a long‑term plan to convert about 70% of adjusted OIBDA into unlevered free cash flow by 2028, Starz is pitching investors a steady, cash‑centric story.

Starz’s earnings call painted a picture of a streamer leaning into profitable growth, disciplined spending, and gradual deleveraging. While linear erosion, franchise concentration, and lower disclosure keep risk on the radar, record OTT subs and improving cash metrics suggest the platform is maturing, making the stock a potential option for investors seeking measured upside rather than hyper‑growth.

data.title: Bladex Calls Virtual 2026 Shareholders’ Meeting, Seeks Approval for Name Change to Bladex, Inc.
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Banco Latinoamericano De Comercio ( (BLX) ) has provided an announcement.

On March 19, 2026, Bladex announced that it will hold its 2026 Annual Meeting of Shareholders virtually on April 21, 2026, at 11:30 a.m. Panama time, accessible via an online platform. Shareholders of all classes will be asked to approve the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025, ratify KPMG as external auditor for 2026, elect three directors for new three-year terms, and cast an advisory vote on executive compensation.

The meeting will also consider amending the Articles of Incorporation to change the bank’s legal name from Banco Latinoamericano de Comercio Exterior, S.A. to Bladex, Inc., signaling a move toward a more streamlined corporate identity. The bank is urging especially beneficial holders of Class E shares to issue voting instructions to their brokers due to NYSE rules on non-routine matters, underscoring that most shares are held in street name and that broad shareholder participation is important for governance decisions at this juncture.

The most recent analyst rating on (BLX) stock is a Buy with a $49.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Banco Latinoamericano De Comercio stock, see the BLX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BLX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BLX is a Outperform.

The score is primarily supported by attractive valuation (low P/E and high dividend yield) and solid profitability/capital metrics, reinforced by a constructive earnings call with reaffirmed guidance and strong deposit and fee-income growth. The main constraints are uneven financial consistency (recent revenue decline and historically volatile cash flows) and only mixed technical momentum (negative MACD despite stable RSI/stochastic).

The bank provides trade-related credit and financial services to corporations, financial institutions, and sovereigns in the region, positioning itself as a specialized intermediary supporting cross-border commerce and regional economic integration.

data.title: Reinsurance Group Adds Peter Babej to Board
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Reinsurance Group ( (RGA) ) has shared an update.

On March 19, 2026, Reinsurance Group of America announced that its board had appointed former Citigroup executive Peter Babej as a director, effective April 1, 2026, for a term running until the 2026 annual meeting of shareholders. The appointment expands the board to 12 members and places a seasoned global financial services specialist at the heart of the reinsurer’s governance.

Babej, 62, brings more than three decades of experience across banking, insurance, and financial advisory, including roles as CEO of Citi Asia Pacific and Chairman and Interim Head of Banking before his 2024 retirement. His background in leading major strategic initiatives, particularly in Asia-Pacific operations and financial institutions, is expected to bolster RGA’s international expansion efforts and support its long-term strategic agenda.

RGA highlighted that Babej has no material related-party transactions with the company and that there were no special arrangements underpinning his appointment to the board. He will receive standard non-employee director compensation, and while he has not yet been assigned to specific board committees, his appointment signals RGA’s focus on strengthening expertise in capital markets and global insurance as it competes in the life and health reinsurance sector.

The most recent analyst rating on (RGA) stock is a Buy with a $264.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Reinsurance Group stock, see the RGA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RGA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RGA is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by solid fundamentals (growth, improved profitability, and a strengthening balance sheet) and a favorable earnings-call outlook (ROE above target and reiterated EPS growth targets). Supportive valuation and a constructive technical setup help, while cash-flow volatility and near-term underwriting/in-force action uncertainty temper the upside.

To see Spark’s full report on RGA stock, click here.

More about Reinsurance Group

Reinsurance Group of America, Incorporated (RGA) is a leading global life and health reinsurer that provides reinsurance and financial solutions to help clients manage risk and optimize capital. Founded in 1973, the company is among the world’s largest reinsurers, with approximately $4.3 trillion of life reinsurance in force and total assets of $156.6 billion as of December 31, 2025.

RGA focuses on life and health reinsurance as well as capital management solutions, positioning itself as a key partner to insurers seeking innovation, risk transfer, and balance sheet efficiency. The group operates worldwide and emphasizes long-term value creation, innovation, and client-centric solutions to expand financial protection access across global insurance markets.

data.title: Scorpius Holdings Raises Costly Short-Term Debt Financing
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Scorpius Holdings ( (SCPX) ) has shared an announcement.

On February 12, 2026, Scorpius Holdings, Inc. issued a non-convertible promissory note for about $30,427 to an institutional investor at 5% interest, maturing by August 12, 2026 or earlier upon certain corporate or default events, and carrying a 15% principal premium on repayment. The note includes cross-default provisions and allows the investor to demand full redemption from proceeds of any subsequent financing.

On February 26, 2026, the company followed with a second non-convertible note of roughly $190,908 on substantially identical terms, maturing by August 26, 2026 and subject to the same 15% premium, default triggers and redemption rights tied to future financings. On March 11, 2026, Scorpius issued a third note of about $27,109 with a September 11, 2026 maturity, again replicating the 5% coupon, 15% premium structure and investor-friendly redemption and cross-default protections.

Collectively, the three short-term notes, all sold in exempt private offerings under Section 4(a)(2) and Regulation D of the Securities Act, provide Scorpius with additional but relatively costly debt capital that increases near-term repayment obligations and grants the holder strong rights over proceeds from any subsequent financing. The structure underscores the company’s pressing liquidity needs and concentrates financing leverage with a single institutional creditor, implications that existing shareholders and other creditors will need to monitor closely.

The most recent analyst rating on (SCPX) stock is a Sell with a $0.01 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Scorpius Holdings stock, see the SCPX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on SCPX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, SCPX is a Underperform.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (declining revenue, persistent losses, negative free cash flow, and high leverage). Technicals also skew bearish with price below key moving averages and negative MACD. Valuation provides limited support because a negative P/E reflects ongoing losses and there is no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on SCPX stock, click here.

More about Scorpius Holdings

Scorpius Holdings, Inc. operates as a publicly traded company that raises capital through institutional debt financing, including non-convertible promissory notes issued in private placements under U.S. securities law exemptions. Its funding approach reflects reliance on structured credit arrangements with institutional investors rather than public equity issuance for near-term liquidity needs.

data.title: Alliant Energy Establishes $1 Billion Stock Distribution Program
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Alliant Energy ( (LNT) ) has issued an update.

On March 19, 2026, Alliant Energy entered into a $1 billion common stock distribution agreement with a syndicate of major banks acting as agents and forward purchasers, enabling it to sell shares from time to time on the Nasdaq Global Select Market or directly to the agents as principals. The structure also allows the company to execute forward confirmations, under which banks borrow and sell shares to hedge future physical settlement, giving Alliant Energy flexible access to equity capital.

The company plans to use net proceeds from any stock sales for general corporate purposes, including potential debt repayment or refinancing, working capital and construction or acquisition spending, which could strengthen its balance sheet and fund ongoing growth initiatives. Because several of the participating banks or their affiliates are already lenders under Alliant Energy’s revolving credit facility, the transaction deepens existing financing relationships and underscores the company’s continued reliance on diversified funding sources in public equity and bank markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (LNT) stock is a Hold with a $74.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Alliant Energy stock, see the LNT Stock Forecast page.

data.description:

Ashford Hospitality ( (AHT) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 17, 2026, Ashford Hospitality Trust completed the sale of the 157-room La Posada de Santa Fe hotel in Santa Fe, New Mexico, for approximately $56.8 million in cash, net of selling expenses, through its indirect subsidiary Ashford Posada LP. The company used roughly $56.0 million of the proceeds to pay down a mortgage secured by La Posada and another hotel, and issued unaudited pro forma financials showing the removal of La Posada’s assets, liabilities, and operating results, including a preliminary, non-recurring gain on the disposition.

The transaction reflects Ashford Hospitality Trust’s continued portfolio reshaping and balance sheet management, as paying down the mortgage debt tied to the asset reduces leverage while crystallizing a one-time gain from the sale. Stakeholders are provided with updated pro forma financial information for 2024 and the nine months ended September 30, 2025, illustrating how the company’s financial position and performance would appear without La Posada, though the company noted the gain and related tax effects remain subject to final adjustment.

The most recent analyst rating on (AHT) stock is a Hold with a $4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ashford Hospitality stock, see the AHT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on AHT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, AHT is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial performance, especially negative equity/high leverage and sustained losses, which outweigh improving free cash flow. data.description:

Banco Bradesco SA ( (BBD) ) has issued an update.

On March 10, 2026, Banco Bradesco held a special shareholders’ meeting that approved a BRL 6.67 billion increase in its stock capital, raising it from BRL 87.1 billion to BRL 93.77 billion through the capitalization of legal profit reserves without issuing new shares. The meeting also endorsed corresponding amendments to the bylaws to reflect the new capital amount and introduced provisions authorizing profit-sharing payments to management at the discretion of the board of directors, signaling a shift in Bradesco’s governance and compensation framework that may affect how profits are allocated between executives and shareholders.

The detailed distance voting results showed broad shareholder support for the capital increase and related bylaw adjustment, while opinions were more divided on allowing profit-sharing for management. These changes strengthen Bradesco’s capital structure via internal resources and formalize a mechanism for variable executive remuneration, which could influence future earnings distribution policy and align management incentives with performance.

The most recent analyst rating on (BBD) stock is a Buy with a $4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Banco Bradesco SA stock, see the BBD Stock Forecast page.

American Shared Hospital Services ( (AMS) ) has provided an update.

American Shared Hospital Services has amended its long-standing Proton Beam Radiation Therapy Lease Agreement with Orlando Health, originally executed in 2006 and previously modified in 2012. On March 13, 2026, the parties agreed to extend the lease for an additional seven years, from April 6, 2026 through April 5, 2033, with lease payments during the extended term tied to a technical component collection percentage that will decrease in certain twelve-month periods.

The amendment also clarifies key operational and financial arrangements related to the leased proton therapy system, including granting Orlando Health an option to purchase the equipment at the end of the lease, with a defined price and exercise window. It further outlines the company’s responsibility and related financial terms for removing the equipment if the purchase option is not exercised, as well as updated maintenance and insurance obligations, providing more certainty for both parties over the long-term use and disposition of the asset.

An update from Tri-Continental Corp ( (TY) ) is now available.

On March 19, 2026, Tri-Continental Corporation’s board announced that the company’s 96th Annual Meeting of Stockholders is scheduled for June 16, 2026, in Minneapolis, Minnesota. The board set April 21, 2026, as the record date for determining stockholders entitled to receive notice of, and vote at, the meeting or any postponement or adjournment.

At the meeting, shareholders will vote on electing four directors—Brian J. Gallagher, Ryan C. Larrenaga, Nancy T. Lukitsh, and Catherine James Paglia—to terms extending to the 2029 annual meeting, and on ratifying PricewaterhouseCoopers LLP as independent auditor for the 2026 fiscal year. These governance decisions will shape the company’s board composition and audit oversight for the coming years, with full details to appear in the forthcoming proxy materials to be filed with regulators.

data.title: Icon Energy Lifts Q1 2026 Revenue Outlook and Bolsters Liquidity Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Icon Energy Corp. ( (ICON) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 19, 2026, Icon Energy Corp. reported that its three-vessel dry bulk fleet is fully employed on index-linked time charters tied to the Baltic Panamax and Supramax indices, aiming to balance high utilization with exposure to freight rate upside. The company highlighted that none of its ships are currently operating in or heading toward the Strait of Hormuz amid heightened U.S.-Iran tensions, while it continues to monitor geopolitical risks and prioritize crew and vessel safety.

For the first quarter of 2026, Icon expects average gross hire of about $14,000 per day, up 63% from $8,600 in the prior-year quarter, supported by the addition of the Ultramax vessel M/V Charlie in June 2025, which is set to lift Operating Days by 50% year on year. As a result, revenue, net for the quarter is projected between $3.5 million and $3.7 million, more than double the $1.5 million recorded in the first quarter of 2025.

Management noted that recent disruptions and uncertainty around commodity flows from the Middle East could tighten vessel availability and support ton-mile demand for dry bulk shipping, potentially underpinning stronger freight markets. While the broader impact of the conflict remains unclear, the company sees the current environment as conducive to improved earnings potential for well-positioned dry bulk owners.

To support growth and strengthen liquidity, Icon disclosed that it has raised $6.9 million in net equity proceeds so far in 2026 through its at-the-market program and a standby equity purchase agreement. The new shares were issued at an average price of $2.82, representing a 188% premium to the last reported Nasdaq Capital Market closing price of its common stock on March 18, 2026, signaling robust pricing despite ongoing market volatility.

The most recent analyst rating on (ICON) stock is a Hold with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Icon Energy Corp. stock, see the ICON Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on ICON Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, ICON is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by weak financial performance: recent losses, sharply higher leverage, and deeply negative free cash flow outweigh improving revenue. Technicals add downside risk as the stock remains in a clear downtrend with negative momentum. Valuation provides little support given the missing P/E and an unusually high reported dividend yield that appears unreliable based on the provided data.

To see Spark’s full report on ICON stock, click here.

More about Icon Energy Corp.

Icon Energy Corp. is an Athens-based international shipping company that provides worldwide seaborne transportation services for dry bulk cargoes via a fleet of oceangoing vessels. The company charters its Panamax, Kamsarmax and Ultramax ships mainly on index-linked or fixed-rate time charters and voyage charters, and its shares trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol ICON.

data.title: Scinai Immunotherapeutics Faces Nasdaq Minimum Bid Price Deficiency, Given Until September to Regain Compliance
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from Scinai Immunotherapeutics ( (SCNI) ) is now available.

On March 16, 2026, Scinai Immunotherapeutics disclosed that it had received a Nasdaq notice on March 12, 2026 stating its American Depositary Shares had traded below the $1.00 minimum bid price for 30 consecutive business days, putting it out of compliance with Nasdaq Capital Market rules. The notification does not immediately affect the listing or trading of the shares, and Scinai has until September 8, 2026 to regain compliance by lifting its ADS closing price to at least $1.00 for ten straight business days, during which it will monitor its stock and consider options to maintain its Nasdaq listing.

The company emphasized that it intends to use the 180‑day grace period provided under Nasdaq’s rules to address the deficiency and avoid potential delisting, a key concern for investors and partners relying on its continued access to U.S. capital markets. Maintaining compliance is important for Scinai’s visibility and liquidity as it develops immunology therapies and offers contract development and manufacturing services through its CDMO subsidiary.

The most recent analyst rating on (SCNI) stock is a Hold with a $0.98 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Scinai Immunotherapeutics stock, see the SCNI Stock Forecast page.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance—persistent operating losses and continued cash burn despite recent balance sheet improvement. data.title: GSK Wins First U.S. Approval for Lynavoy in PBC-Related Itch, Advancing Hepatology Push
data.description:
Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest announcement is out from GlaxoSmithKline ( (GSK) ).

On 19 March 2026, GSK announced that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved Lynavoy (linerixibat), an ileal bile acid transporter inhibitor, as the first medicine in the U.S. specifically indicated for cholestatic pruritus in adults with primary biliary cholangitis, a rare autoimmune liver disease. The decision, underpinned by positive phase III GLISTEN trial data and orphan drug designations, marks GSK’s first liver therapy approval, strengthens its position in hepatology just days after agreeing to license global rights to linerixibat to Alfasigma, and could significantly improve quality of life for PBC patients as regulatory reviews continue in Europe, the U.K., Canada and China.

The most recent analyst rating on (GSK) stock is a Hold with a $58.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GlaxoSmithKline stock, see the GSK Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GSK Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GSK is a Neutral.

The score is supported primarily by resilient fundamentals (strong profitability and positive cash generation) and a constructive earnings outlook with higher dividend guidance and continued specialty/HIV momentum. These positives are tempered by leverage and recent revenue/FCF volatility, while technically the stock is in a strong uptrend but looks overbought, raising near-term pullback risk.

To see Spark’s full report on GSK stock, click here.

More about GlaxoSmithKline

GSK plc is a global biopharmaceutical company that develops and commercialises prescription medicines and vaccines, with a growing focus on immune‑mediated and liver diseases. The group is extending its inflammation expertise into hepatology, advancing a pipeline that targets chronic hepatitis B and steatotic liver disease, including metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis and alcohol-associated liver disease.
